text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
The Kikuka Sho, the final hurdle of the Classic
Triple Crown, is here at last.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro McQueen
text_block_list.text:
You've done well, Bright. Of the Triple Crown
races, the Kikuka Sho is said to be won by the
strongest Umamusume, in other words, the one
with the most formidable skill.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro McQueen
text_block_list.text:
Tricks won't get you through this one. Trust in
yourself and win by your ability.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text:
I will, McQueen. With what you've taught me, I
am sure—
HOLD ON!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Suddenly, a voice reverberates throughout the
runners' tunnel.

The Kikuka Sho is not won by the strongest
Umamusume! In reality...

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
It is won by the luckiest! Lucky girl
Matikanefukukitaru has arrived!

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text:
Oh! Hello, Fukukitaru. I didn't know you were also
running today.

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
Well, now you do know! The Kikuka Sho is my
destiny!

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
Long-distance races are like board games! And
board games mean dice! And dice mean luck!

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
In other words, today's race is a matter of luck!
Heheh!

text_block_list.name: Mejiro McQueen
text_block_list.text:
I've certainly never heard that before. To suggest
the Kikuka Sho is determined by luck...

text_block_list.name: Mejiro McQueen
text_block_list.text: What then of the Japanese Derby, which actually is said to be won by the luckiest? What matters most in that race, then?

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text: Luck!

text_block_list.name: Mejiro McQueen
text_block_list.text:
Then what about the race said to be won by the
fastest Umamusume, the Satsuki Sho?

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text: Luck!

text_block_list.name: Mejiro McQueen
text_block_list.text: So they're all determined by luck?!

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
Exactly! If you've got luck on your side, you can
win any race! You can do anything!

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
Bright, I will beat you fair and square! No aces up
my sleeve, just pure good fortune!

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text: Okaaay. See you on the turf!

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text: Why do you say that, Trainer?
choice_data_list:
I don't think Fukukitaru's really relying on luck
alone.

I don't think Fukukitaru's really relying on luck
alone.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I warn her about how well Matikanefukukitaru has
performed this fall.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
She's been achieving decisive, consecutive
victories in preliminary races. She may call it luck,
but when you see her run, you can tell she's just
entering her prime.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text:
Yes, I'm already aware. She's become a
formidable opponent.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
However, I'm not sure if Mejiro Bright's sleeper
stayer abilities will be enough to compete against
a runner in her prime...
